key_insight: Scanning 3DXRD combined with crystal plasticity reveals how rapid thermomechanical cycling in Cu metallization concentrates grain-resolved plasticity into potential degradation hotspots.
engineering_relevance: The study helps explain and predict reliability risks in power semiconductor metallization during short-circuit events, supporting microstructure-informed lifetime assessment.
method: Performed scanning 3D X-ray diffraction to measure grain-resolved deformation in Cu metallization.
Coupled the measurements with thermomechanical crystal plasticity simulations.
Tracked plastic deformation evolution under rapid cyclic loading to identify hotspot formation mechanisms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a combined experimental-computational analysis of grain-resolved plastic deformation evolution under rapid cyclic loading, rather than a single global failure metric.
watch_item: The approach is highly data- and microstructure-dependent, so transferring the results to other metallization geometries or loading histories may require careful calibration and validation.
paper_title: Subgrain-resolved Analysis of Degradation in Cu Metallization via Scanning 3DXRD and Thermomechanical Modeling
